channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sun, 19 Jul 2026 12:46:34 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Mount Olympus, the mythological home of Zeus and the ancient Greek gods, is being considered for UNESCO World Heritage status as Greece seeks protection for its tallest mountain
item.description: Mount Olympus, a site of myth and nature, seeks UNESCO World Heritage recognition. This ancient Greek mountain is home to gods and diverse ecosystems. Locals hope for global acknowledgment to ensure its continued protection. The nomination process involves careful review and potential further requirements. Recognition would bring responsibilities for preserving its unique cultural and natural values.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is WardrobeWinter? Meet Plaqueboymax’s roommate at Kai Cenat’s Streamer University 2026
item.description: WardrobeWinter became one of the biggest breakout creators from Kai Cenat’s Streamer University 2026. Already popular on TikTok and Twitch, he drew even more attention after his roommate clashes with Plaqueboymax. With help from counsellor YourRAGE, the two talked through their problems, agreed on simple ground rules and eventually ended the situation with a hug.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: US launches fresh airstrikes on Iran after attack on American troops in Jordan
item.description: United States forces launched new airstrikes against Iran on President Donald Trump's orders. These strikes aim to degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping. The operation also retaliates for recent attacks on American service members in Jordan. The US Central Command confirmed the military action began at 6 p.m. ET. Iran has not yet issued any official comment on the developments.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Indian-origin tech leader Uday Ruddaraju, who has become OpenAI's CTO of Compute
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Jimmy Carter: 'We should live our lives as though Christ were coming this afternoon'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Streamer University 2026 ending date explained as Kai Cenat reveals what happens before July 20
item.description: The grand finale of Streamer University 2026, hosted by Kai Cenat, is set for July 20th morning. Alumni have begun to flock to campus to engage with students in these last days of festivities. Kai has made it clear that any rule infractions could lead to expulsion from campus. This year's event boasts a longer duration than its predecessor, designed to maximize creative collaboration and ensure a vibrant atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Boris Johnson: 'My policy on cake is pro having it and pro eating it' and how it became a key phrase of Brexit
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Are you comfortable with daily late evening calls?' Techie who returned to India from US shocked to see this question on online application
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Socrates' student Plato: "One of the penalties for refusing to participate in politics is…"
item.description: Plato's ancient warning highlights the consequences of political disengagement. Choosing not to participate means others will make decisions for you. Withdrawal does not exempt individuals from the outcomes of governance. Informed citizens must remain engaged to hold power accountable. This ensures that capable individuals, not just ambitious ones, shape society.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iran Supreme Leader's message to US: Mojtaba Khamenei calls Trump's signature worthless, issues new warning
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader warned the United States of "unforgettable lessons" if attacks continued. He declared President Trump's signature "worthless and invalid" amid escalating conflict. Tehran suspended commitments under a recent interim agreement, citing US breaches. Both nations exchanged strikes on military and infrastructure targets. Regional security and global energy supplies remain a concern.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: After paying $32000 tuition fee for 2 years, Indian students in Canada told they are not eligible for post-graduation work permit
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Sister of Indian woman stabbed to death in London demands justice
'Netanyahu belongs in the Hague': Mamdani considering Israel PM's arrest during NYC visit
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Largest residential fire in Norway's modern history destroys at least 100 homes
item.description: A massive fire destroyed at least one hundred homes in Drammen, Norway. Hundreds of residents were forced to evacuate their residences after the blaze spread rapidly. Firefighters worked through Saturday to bring the extensive fire under control. Helicopters were deployed to assist in dousing the flames with water. The cause of this historic residential fire remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin woman jailed in UK for more than 2 years over Rs 2.8 crore Covid loan fraud
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Blood for blood': Iran displays giant billboard with Donald, Melania and Trump children in coffins
item.description: A new billboard in Tehran shows Donald Trump and his family above coffins draped in the US flag. The prominent slogan 'Blood for Blood' signals Iran's intent for revenge against recent strikes. This display follows other murals targeting Trump, including one showing him in an open coffin. These actions occur as military tensions between Iran and the United States intensify.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Confusion, lack of equipment hindered Venezuela's response to earthquake that killed over 5,000: Report
item.description: Venezuelan military response to earthquakes faced significant delays. Poor coordination and delayed deployment orders slowed rescue efforts. Essential equipment shortages further hampered the armed forces' capabilities. Civilians and international teams performed initial rescue work. Economic crisis years eroded the military's operational readiness.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US Air Force airman killed in heavy equipment accident at California space base
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian woman seeks help in getting F-1 visa appointment, Bill Ackman responds and receives massive backlash: 'Why don't you finance a Harvard in Kerala'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Famous chimpanzees solved puzzles that changed how humans understood intelligence, but their move to Berlin had a tragic ending
item.description: Pioneering chimpanzee experiments revealed advanced intelligence and problem-solving skills. These apes demonstrated insight, challenging human uniqueness in reasoning. After their research, the chimpanzees faced harsh conditions in Berlin. Their welfare declined, and their lives were largely overlooked by history. New research highlights their forgotten stories and ethical responsibilities in science.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
